description: Make the OpenTabs docs site fully mobile-ready by applying the same responsive patterns used in fumadocs-dev. Every change must preserve the desktop experience — mobile-only improvements using responsive breakpoints, media queries, and mobile-first CSS.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Homepage mobile navigation with collapsible menu
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want a collapsible navigation menu on the homepage so that I can navigate to docs, components, and guides without scrolling back to find links.
acceptanceCriteria: A chevron/hamburger toggle button appears in the homepage header on screens below lg (1024px)
Clicking the toggle opens a collapsible panel below the header with navigation links to key pages (Docs, Get Started)
The collapsible panel includes the theme toggle and search button on mobile
Desktop navigation (above lg) is completely unchanged — links appear inline as before
The mobile menu closes when a link is clicked (navigation occurs)
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
The homepage uses Fumadocs HomeLayout which has its own nav component pattern. Currently `docs/lib/layout.shared.tsx` sets `nav.component: <GlobalHeader />` which replaces the entire nav. The homepage GlobalHeader has no mobile menu — just search + theme toggle.

Fumadocs-dev implements this in `packages/base-ui/src/layouts/home/client.tsx` with a `MobileMenuCollapsible` pattern: a `Collapsible` from Base UI wrapping a `CollapsibleTrigger` (visible `lg:hidden`) and `CollapsibleContent` containing mobile nav links. Links use `sm:hidden` to only show on very small screens, with secondary items (icon buttons) always visible.

For our docs site, the approach should be:
1. Create a new `docs/components/home-header.tsx` (or modify GlobalHeader to accept nav items) that renders a collapsible menu on mobile.
2. The homepage header should show: Logo | [spacer] | Search (mobile icon) | Menu Toggle (lg:hidden) on mobile, and Logo | Nav Links | Search Bar | Theme Toggle on desktop.
3. Use Radix Collapsible (`@radix-ui/react-collapsible` is already installed) for the mobile panel.
4. The collapsible content should contain: links to /docs, /docs/guides/installation, and the theme toggle + search icon.
5. Pattern: `lg:hidden` for the toggle trigger, `max-lg:hidden` for desktop-only nav items.
6. Click outside or navigation should close the menu.

Key files:
- `docs/components/global-header.tsx` — current header (may need a home-specific variant)
- `docs/lib/layout.shared.tsx` — baseOptions used by HomeLayout
- `docs/app/page.tsx` — homepage using HomeLayout with baseOptions

userStories.title: Responsive hero section typography and spacing
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the homepage hero section to be readable and well-proportioned on small screens so that the headline and description don't feel cramped or oversized.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero heading scales smoothly: text-2xl on mobile, sm:text-4xl on small tablets, md:text-7xl on desktop (already exists but verify line-height is tight)
The 'Now in beta' badge has adequate touch-friendly padding (min-h of 2.5rem / 40px on mobile)
Hero CTA buttons stack vertically on very small screens (below sm) and sit side-by-side above sm
The highlighted span ('any web app') with bg-primary has adequate inline padding on mobile (px-1 minimum) so text doesn't touch the yellow background edge
Maximum content width is 100% minus safe-area insets on mobile (content doesn't touch screen edges)
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
The hero section is in `docs/app/page.tsx` lines 46-68. Current responsive classes are mostly correct but need fine-tuning:

1. The heading `text-2xl sm:text-4xl md:text-7xl` is good but `leading-tight` may need `sm:leading-tight md:leading-none` for tighter tracking at large sizes.
2. The CTA buttons use `flex-wrap gap-4` which handles wrapping, but on very small screens (< 360px) the buttons should be `w-full` to prevent awkward partial wrapping. Add `max-sm:w-full` to the Button children or wrap in a `flex-col sm:flex-row` container.
3. The 'any web app' span with `bg-primary px-2` is fine on desktop but on mobile the px-2 works. Just verify it doesn't clip.
4. The overall section uses `px-4 sm:px-6` which provides 1rem/1.5rem side padding — this is correct and matches fumadocs-dev's pattern.

Key file: `docs/app/page.tsx` lines 46-68

userStories.title: Feature cards and How It Works grid mobile spacing
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the feature cards and how-it-works steps to have appropriate spacing and shadow sizes on small screens so they don't feel heavy or crowded.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature cards use shadow-sm on mobile (max-sm) instead of shadow-md to reduce visual weight on narrow screens
Feature card padding is p-4 on mobile and sm:p-6 on larger screens
How It Works step number badges are appropriately sized on mobile
Gap between cards is gap-4 on mobile and sm:gap-6 on larger screens
All feature card text remains readable — no text is clipped or overflowing
Desktop card appearance is completely unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
Feature cards are in `docs/app/page.tsx` lines 76-85. The current classes on each card are:
```
border-border bg-card flex h-full flex-col gap-3 rounded border-2 p-6 shadow-md transition-all hover:translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow
```

Changes needed:
1. Change `p-6` to `p-4 sm:p-6` — tighter padding on mobile
2. Change `shadow-md` to `shadow-sm sm:shadow-md` — lighter shadow on mobile (4px offset is heavy on 320px screens)
3. Change `gap-6` on the grid to `gap-4 sm:gap-6` — tighter spacing on mobile

Same pattern for How It Works cards at lines 94-120:
1. Change `p-6` to `p-4 sm:p-6`
2. Change `shadow-md` to `shadow-sm sm:shadow-md`
3. Change `gap-6` to `gap-4 sm:gap-6`

Also the step number badge `size-12` (48px) is fine on mobile but could be `size-10 sm:size-12` for a slightly more compact look. The text-xl can become `text-lg sm:text-xl`.

Key file: `docs/app/page.tsx`

userStories.title: Mobile sidebar drawer polish and safe-area handling
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the sidebar drawer to have proper safe-area insets, smooth animations, and a clean overlay so it feels native and doesn't clip on notch devices.
acceptanceCriteria: The sidebar drawer overlay has backdrop-blur effect (backdrop-blur-xs) matching fumadocs-dev
The sidebar drawer content area has overscroll-contain to prevent scroll chaining to the body
The sidebar drawer respects safe-area-inset-right for notch devices (already in global.css line 249)
The sidebar drawer has safe-area-inset-bottom padding so the last item isn't hidden behind the home indicator
Sidebar drawer scrollable content area has -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch for momentum scrolling on iOS
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
The mobile sidebar drawer is rendered by Fumadocs UI's `SidebarDrawerContent` and `SidebarDrawerOverlay` components. Our CSS overrides are in `docs/app/global.css` lines 246-250.

Fumadocs-dev's drawer implementation (`packages/base-ui/src/layouts/docs/sidebar.tsx` lines 120-139):
- Overlay: `fixed z-40 inset-0 backdrop-blur-xs data-[state=open]:animate-fd-fade-in data-[state=closed]:animate-fd-fade-out`
- Content: `fixed text-[0.9375rem] flex flex-col shadow-lg border-s end-0 inset-y-0 w-[85%] max-w-[380px] z-40 bg-fd-background`
- Sidebar viewport has `overscroll-contain` (`packages/base-ui/src/components/sidebar/base.tsx` line 230)

Changes needed in `docs/app/global.css`:
1. Add `overscroll-behavior: contain` to `#nd-sidebar-mobile` to prevent body scroll when scrolling the sidebar
2. Add `padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px)` to `#nd-sidebar-mobile` for home indicator
3. Add `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` for momentum scrolling (though modern WebKit defaults to this)

Existing rules to keep:
- `border-left-width: 2px` (line 248)
- `padding-right: env(safe-area-inset-right, 0px)` (line 249)

Key file: `docs/app/global.css` lines 246-250

userStories.title: Mobile TOC popover improvements
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the table of contents popover to be easy to use on touch devices with proper styling and safe-area handling.
acceptanceCriteria: TOC popover has a solid background (no backdrop-blur transparency) matching the RetroUI style
TOC popover trigger has adequate touch target height (min-h-10 / 40px)
TOC popover content scrollable area has overscroll-contain to prevent scroll chaining
TOC popover respects safe-area insets on mobile (already in global.css lines 497-502)
TOC popover border styling matches the RetroUI 2px pattern (already in global.css lines 399-404 and 490-493)
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
The TOC popover is rendered by Fumadocs UI and styled via CSS overrides in `docs/app/global.css`.

Existing CSS overrides:
- Lines 399-404: header has `border-bottom-width: 2px`, `backdrop-filter: none`, `background-color: var(--background)` (solid bg, no blur)
- Lines 490-493: `[data-toc-popover]` has `border-bottom-width: 2px`
- Lines 497-502: safe area insets for trigger and content on `max-width: 767px`

Fumadocs-dev's TOC popover (`packages/base-ui/src/layouts/docs/page/client.tsx` lines 63-84):
- Container: `sticky top-(--fd-docs-row-2) z-10 h-(--fd-toc-popover-height) xl:hidden max-xl:layout:[--fd-toc-popover-height:--spacing(10)]`
- Trigger: `flex w-full h-10 items-center text-sm text-fd-muted-foreground gap-2.5 px-4 py-2.5`
- Content: `flex flex-col px-4 max-h-[50vh] md:px-6`

Changes needed:
1. Add `overscroll-behavior: contain` to the TOC popover scrollable content so that scrolling to the end of the TOC doesn't scroll the page behind it
2. Add `max-height: 50vh` to the TOC popover content to prevent it from taking the full screen
3. Ensure the popover content area has proper padding that matches the trigger

Add these CSS rules targeting `[data-toc-popover-content]`:
```css
[data-toc-popover-content] {
  max-height: 50vh;
  overscroll-behavior: contain;
}
```

Key file: `docs/app/global.css` around lines 490-503

userStories.title: Responsive MDX content typography and tables
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want MDX content (headings, tables, code blocks, callouts) to be properly sized and scrollable on small screens without horizontal overflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Tables scroll horizontally within their container without causing page-level horizontal scroll
Table cells have tighter padding on mobile: px-2 py-2 on mobile, px-4 py-3 on md+
Table header cells have tighter padding on mobile: px-2 on mobile, px-4 on md+
Inline code elements use break-words to prevent long tokens from overflowing (already present)
Callout padding is responsive: p-3 on mobile, md:p-5 on desktop (currently p-4 md:p-5)
Blockquote has responsive padding: px-3 on mobile, md:px-4 on desktop
Desktop typography and spacing is completely unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
MDX component overrides are in `docs/mdx-components.tsx`. Current responsive patterns:

1. **Tables** (lines 163-182): The wrapper has `overflow-auto rounded border-2 shadow-lg`. Table cells use fixed `px-4 py-3` and headers use `h-12 px-4`. On mobile screens (320-375px wide), 4-column tables with px-4 cells can be very wide. Changes:
   - `th`: Change `px-4` to `px-2 md:px-4` and `h-12` to `h-10 md:h-12`
   - `td`: Change `px-4 py-3` to `px-2 py-2 md:px-4 md:py-3`
   - Table wrapper shadow: Change `shadow-lg` to `shadow-sm sm:shadow-lg` (large shadows on mobile overflow)

2. **Callouts** (line 33): Currently `p-4 md:p-5`. Change to `p-3 md:p-5` for slightly tighter mobile padding. The font-sans text-sm is fine.

3. **Blockquotes** (lines 131-138): Currently `px-4 py-3`. Change to `px-3 py-2 md:px-4 md:py-3` for tighter mobile padding.

4. **Code blocks** (`docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx`): The viewport has `max-h-[400px] sm:max-h-[600px]` and `overflow-auto` which handles mobile well. The `p-4` padding could be `p-3 sm:p-4` for slightly less padding on mobile.

Key files:
- `docs/mdx-components.tsx` lines 28-182
- `docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx` lines 83-139

userStories.title: Mobile-optimized code blocks and copy button touch targets
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want code blocks to be easy to read and interact with on small screens, with appropriately sized copy buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Code block viewport padding is p-3 on mobile and sm:p-4 on larger screens
Floating copy button (no title) has min-h-10 min-w-10 (40px) touch target on mobile for easy tapping
Title bar copy button remains its current size (constrained by the title bar height)
Code block figure margin is my-4 on mobile and sm:my-6 on larger screens to save vertical space
Code block shadow is shadow-sm on mobile and sm:shadow-md on larger screens
Desktop code block appearance is completely unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
Code blocks are in `docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx`.

Changes needed:

1. **Viewport padding** (line 132): Change `p-4` to `p-3 sm:p-4`

2. **Floating copy button** (line 117): The button container is `absolute top-2 right-2 z-10 sm:top-3 sm:right-3`. The button itself (RetroCopyButton) has `p-1.5` with `size-3.5` icons. On mobile, add a responsive min-size:
   - In the floating copy div wrapper, no change needed to position
   - On the RetroCopyButton component, the className already accepts merging. When rendered as floating (no title), pass an additional class like `max-sm:min-h-10 max-sm:min-w-10 max-sm:p-2` to make it a larger touch target on mobile. But be careful: the CLAUDE.md says not to apply blanket sizing rules inside constrained containers. The floating button is NOT inside a constrained container (it's absolutely positioned), so this is safe. The title-bar button IS inside a constrained container (min-h-10 title bar), so do NOT change that one.

3. **Figure margins** (line 92): Change `my-6` to `my-4 sm:my-6`

4. **Shadow** (line 92): Change `shadow-md` to `shadow-sm sm:shadow-md`

5. **Line padding override** (line 79): The `--padding-right` calc uses `var(--spacing) * 10`. On mobile with the larger copy button, this might need to be slightly wider. Consider `calc(var(--spacing) * 12)` for mobile or just keep as is since the copy button overlaps the code slightly.

Key file: `docs/components/retroui-codeblock.tsx`

userStories.title: Responsive accordion, tabs, steps, and files components
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want interactive MDX components (accordions, tabs, steps, file trees) to be properly sized and usable on touch devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Accordion trigger has min-h-11 (44px) touch target (already present)
Accordion content padding is responsive: px-3 pb-3 on mobile, md:px-4 md:pb-4
Tab triggers have adequate touch-friendly height and the tab list scrolls horizontally on mobile (already present)
Steps component left margin and padding is responsive (already present: ml-2 pl-7 md:ml-4 md:pl-10)
File tree items have min-h-10 (40px) touch targets on mobile
File tree folder triggers have adequate touch target height
Desktop appearance of all components is completely unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
These components are in separate files under `docs/components/`.

1. **Accordion** (`docs/components/retro-accordion.tsx`):
   - Trigger (line 33): Already has `min-h-11` (44px) — good.
   - Content inner div (line 39): `px-4 pt-2 pb-4`. Change to `px-3 pt-2 pb-3 md:px-4 md:pb-4`.

2. **Tabs** (`docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx`):
   - Tab list (line 74): Already has `overflow-x-auto flex-nowrap` with hidden scrollbar — good for mobile.
   - Tab trigger (lines 77-84): `px-4 py-1`. These are adequate. Could add `min-h-9` for slightly taller touch targets but the whitespace-nowrap prevents wrapping which is correct.
   - Tab content (line 114): `p-4`. Change to `p-3 sm:p-4`.

3. **Steps** (`docs/components/retro-steps.tsx`):
   - Already responsive: `ml-2 pl-7 md:ml-4 md:pl-10` — matches fumadocs-dev pattern exactly. No changes needed.

4. **Files** (`docs/components/retro-files.tsx`):
   - File items (line 24): `px-2 py-1.5` — somewhat small for touch. Add `min-h-9` for a 36px minimum height.
   - Folder triggers (line 54): `px-2 py-1.5` — same, add `min-h-9`.
   - The 36px (min-h-9) is slightly below the 44px guideline but appropriate for dense lists where users can see many items. Full 44px would make the file tree very tall.

Key files:
- `docs/components/retro-accordion.tsx`
- `docs/components/retro-tabs.tsx`
- `docs/components/retro-files.tsx`
- `docs/components/retro-steps.tsx` (no changes needed)

userStories.title: Mobile page footer card stacking and touch targets
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the previous/next page navigation cards to stack vertically on mobile and have easy-to-tap touch targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Previous and Next cards stack to full width on screens below md (768px) using max-md:col-span-full
Footer cards have min-h-16 (64px) on mobile for easy touch targets
Footer card shadows are shadow-sm on mobile and sm:shadow-md on larger screens
Footer card padding is responsive: p-3 on mobile, sm:p-4 on larger screens
Grid gap is responsive: gap-3 on mobile, sm:gap-4 on larger screens
Desktop footer appearance is completely unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
The page footer is in `docs/components/retro-page-footer.tsx`.

Current code (lines 30-57):
- Grid: `mt-6 grid gap-4 border-t-2 pt-4 sm:mt-8 sm:pt-6` with `grid-cols-2` or `grid-cols-1`
- Previous card: `flex flex-col gap-2 rounded border-2 p-4 shadow-md ... max-lg:col-span-full`
- Next card: `flex flex-col gap-2 rounded border-2 p-4 text-end shadow-md ... max-lg:col-span-full`

Changes needed:
1. Change `max-lg:col-span-full` to `max-md:col-span-full` on both cards — on tablets (768-1023px) the two-column layout works fine. Only stack on mobile.
2. Change card `p-4` to `p-3 sm:p-4`
3. Change card `shadow-md` to `shadow-sm sm:shadow-md`
4. Change grid `gap-4` to `gap-3 sm:gap-4`
5. Add `min-h-16` to cards for mobile touch target (64px is generous for a nav card)

Actually, re-examining: `max-lg:col-span-full` means they stack below 1024px. Fumadocs-dev uses `@max-lg:col-span-full` (container query). For our site, `max-md:col-span-full` (below 768px) is more appropriate since the cards are wide enough on tablets.

Key file: `docs/components/retro-page-footer.tsx`

userStories.title: Mobile breadcrumb overflow and touch target improvements
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want breadcrumbs to handle long paths gracefully on narrow screens without causing horizontal overflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Breadcrumb container uses flex-wrap (already present) so items wrap to next line on narrow screens
Breadcrumb links have adequate touch target size: min-h-8 (32px) via py padding
Long breadcrumb item names are truncated with text-ellipsis on mobile (max-w-[150px] max-sm:max-w-[100px])
Chevron separators are properly aligned with wrapped text
Desktop breadcrumb appearance is unchanged
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
userStories.notes:
The breadcrumb is in `docs/components/retro-page-breadcrumb.tsx`.

Current code (lines 18-41):
- Container: `flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1.5 text-sm` — wrapping is correct
- Links: `hover:text-foreground font-medium transition-colors`
- Current page: `truncate font-semibold` — already truncates the current page

Changes needed:
1. Add `max-w-[200px] truncate` to breadcrumb links (line 26-30) so long page names don't cause the breadcrumb to span multiple lines. Use `max-sm:max-w-[120px]` for very narrow screens.
2. Add `py-1` to breadcrumb links for slightly taller touch targets (the text-sm links are about 20px tall currently — adding py-1 makes them 28px which is adequate for secondary navigation).
3. The gap-1.5 (6px) is tight on mobile. Consider gap-1 on mobile and sm:gap-1.5 on larger screens. Actually the current gap is fine since items wrap.

Key file: `docs/components/retro-page-breadcrumb.tsx`
